_comment: Match rules for the supplier entity type.
Suppliers have a stable vendor code (vendor_id) assigned in the source ERP.
Intacct and X3 are separate company environments so the same real-world
supplier may have different codes in each system.  Without a shared external
ID (e.g. tax ID, D-U-N-S), deterministic matching alone cannot reliably
Phase 5 approach: deterministic match on vendor_id within a single source.
Cross-source fuzzy matching on full_name is included as a probabilistic
rule for candidate generation; downstream analysts review low-confidence
matches before golden records are produced.
Blocking: RECORD_ID_PREFIX on vendor_id bounds intra-source block sizes
while the name-based fuzzy rule uses a separate token-block strategy.
When a third supplier source is integrated (e.g. NetSuite), add it to
source_priority in survivorship_v1.json.  No code changes required.

blocking._comment: Partition by first 8 chars of vendor_id for intra-source deterministic matching.

rules._comment: Rule 1 — Deterministic: same vendor_id within the same source = same supplier.
Rule 2 — Probabilistic: normalised name similarity for cross-source candidate matching.
